title: ARDC engagement: IPv6 allocation, grant application, tunnel network
description:
Comprehensive engagement with ARDC (Amateur Radio Digital Communications) for LICHEN mesh network support.

## What to Ask For

### 1. IPv6 Address Allocation
- Request: /48 from amateur radio IPv6 space (44::/16 when IANA approves)
- Use: Border routers operated by licensed amateurs
- Justification: Standards-based IPv6 mesh (RPL/CoAP/SCHC), Part 97 compliant
- Benefit: Real GUA addresses, global routing between mesh islands

### 2. Grant Funding (50k-150k initial ask)
- Hardware for 3-5 regional test deployments (10-50 nodes each)
- Developer time for border router hardening
- Documentation: operator guide, Part 97 compliance brief, deployment playbook
- Integration with ARDC IPv6 allocation infrastructure

### 3. AMPRNet Tunnel Network Access
- Connect LICHEN border routers to AMPRNet backbone
- Bridge disconnected mesh islands via 44net routing
- Participate in ham IP routing ecosystem

### 4. Recognition/Partnership
- Listed as ARDC-supported project
- Access to ARDC technical community
- Potential conference presentation (Hamvention, etc.)

## Prerequisites (must complete before asking)
- Working border router implementation
- Part 97 compliance documentation
- Real-world test deployment (5-10 nodes)
- Published spec and implementation
- At least one licensed amateur running nodes

## The Pitch
LICHEN is a standards-based IPv6 mesh for LoRa. IPv6/RPL/CoAP/SCHC/OSCORE stack (not proprietary). Auth-only security: Schnorr signatures for link auth, payload readable (Part 97 compliant). Working Zephyr and Python implementation. Designed for SAR, EMCOMM, field ops.

## Gate
DEFERRED. Do not proceed until explicitly told we are ready to ask ARDC.
notes: Gate: DEFERRED until explicitly told 'we are now going to ask ARDC' or equivalent. Prerequisites are necessary but not sufficient—timing and relationship-building matter. Supersedes lora-ipv6-mesh-syo.
